qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unauthenticated information disclosure via public settings endpoint exposes security configuration
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding concerns unauthenticated disclosure from GET /rest/settings and frontend public settings serialization, while the advisory concerns prototype pollution through XML and GSuiteAdmin node parameters leading to RCE.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Server-Side Request Forgery in workflow import-from-url endpoint bypasses SSRF protection
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding concerns a raw axios.get call in the workflow import-from-url REST endpoint without SSRF protection, while the advisory concerns prototype pollution in XML and GSuiteAdmin node parameter handling.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described prototype pollution root cause in XML or GSuiteAdmin node parameters. The two qualifying findings are separate open-world issues in a public settings endpoint and a workflow import-from-url SSRF endpoint.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-mxrg-77hm-89hv describes an authenticated workflow creator or editor supplying crafted node configuration parameters to pollute Object.prototype through XML and GSuiteAdmin node handling, with possible remote code execution.

Finding settings-information-disclosure identifies GET /rest/settings configured with allowUnauthenticated=true and returning public frontend settings; it does not involve node parameter parsing, Object.prototype writes, XML, GSuiteAdmin, or RCE.

Finding ssrf-workflows-from-url identifies WorkflowsController.getFromUrl calling axios.get on an attacker-controlled URL without SsrfProtectionService; it does not involve prototype pollution, XML/GSuiteAdmin parameter handling, or the advisory RCE chain.
